findings.message: New dependency 'requests' is added without any version constraint. Every other runtime dependency in this manifest specifies a minimum version (e.g., '>=2.x'). An unpinned 'requests' allows the resolver to select any version, including old releases with known CVEs (e.g., credential leakage via redirects, SSRF via malformed URLs).
findings.suggestion: Add a minimum version floor, e.g. "requests>=2.32" (the first release that addresses the urllib3 CVE-2024-37891 / requests CVE-2024-35195 credential-leakage chain).

findings.message: 'requests' is a synchronous HTTP library; this project is an async FastAPI service. Blocking calls to 'requests' inside async route handlers or Celery-less background tasks will block the event loop. 'httpx' (already present in the dev group) supports both sync and async modes and is the idiomatic choice for async FastAPI services.
findings.suggestion: Consider replacing 'requests' with 'httpx>=0.28' promoted to the runtime dependencies group, unless 'requests' is intentionally restricted to a non-async context (e.g., a CLI script or a Celery worker task that runs in its own thread).

findings.message: No justification is provided for why 'requests' was added. All other dependencies in the manifest map to clearly identifiable framework roles (ORM, observability, circuit-breaker, etc.). Without a comment or PR description explaining the use case, it is difficult to assess whether an existing dependency (httpx, FastAPI's TestClient which wraps requests, etc.) already covers the need.
findings.suggestion: Add an inline comment (e.g., '# used for <use-case>') or confirm in the PR description what functionality requires 'requests' that 'httpx' or another existing dependency cannot provide.
